index.title: Odoo–Shopify Order Check
index.description: Odoo–Shopify Order Check - Compare any order between Odoo and Shopify field by field, get an instant verdict and a clear discrepancy breakdown. Read-only Chrome extension.
index.ogDescription: Catch order mismatches between Odoo and Shopify before they cost you. Field-by-field comparison, a verdict based on what's invoiced, and a discrepancy breakdown — read-only.
index.twitterDescription: Field-by-field order comparison between Odoo and Shopify, with an instant verdict and discrepancy breakdown. Read-only.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy - Odoo Integration for Gmail
privacy.description: Privacy Policy for Odoo Integration for Gmail. Learn how we handle your data, what permissions we request, and your privacy rights.
privacy.ogTitle: Privacy Policy - Odoo Integration for Gmail
privacy.ogDescription: Learn how we handle your data, what permissions we request, and your privacy rights.
privacy.twitterTitle: Privacy Policy - Odoo Integration for Gmail
privacy.twitterDescription: Learn how we handle your data and your privacy rights.

header.title: User Guide
header.subtitle: Everything you need to know to get the most out of Odoo Integration for Gmail

installation.title: Installation
installation.odooVersion: Version 14.0 or higher (on-premise, Odoo.sh, or Odoo Online Custom plan)
installation.step1Desc: Go to Odoo Integration for Gmail or search for "Odoo Integration for Gmail" in Chrome Web Store.
installation.step2Desc: Click the "Add to Chrome" button and review the permissions requested.
installation.step3Desc: You'll see a confirmation message and the extension icon will appear in your Chrome toolbar.
installation.step4Desc: Click the puzzle icon in Chrome toolbar, find "Odoo Integration for Gmail", and click the pin icon.
installation.noteDesc: If you see a message saying "This extension is not trusted by Enhanced Safe Browsing", this is normal for newly published extensions. The extension has been reviewed and approved by Chrome Web Store.

configuration.title: Configuration
configuration.step1Desc: Navigate to mail.google.com and open any email to view the sidebar.
configuration.step2Desc: Make sure you're logged in to your Odoo instance in the same browser — the extension connects using your active session.
configuration.step3Desc: Enter your Odoo instance URL (e.g., https://yourcompany.odoo.com). Use HTTPS and don't include a trailing slash.
configuration.step4Desc: Click Connect and the extension verifies your active session. An API key is optional — only needed for background auto-logging, or as a fallback if your session expires.
configuration.updateDesc: To change your Odoo URL, log out and reconnect:
configuration.updateStep1: Click the menu button (three vertical dots ⋮) in the sidebar
configuration.versionNote: Adding an API key is optional. If you add one, the steps to create it vary between Odoo versions.
configuration.versionNoteLink: See the detailed guide for your version →
configuration.updateApiKeyNote: To add or update your API key later, open Settings → Account → Connection — no need to log out.

free.contactViewingDesc: View Odoo partner information directly in Gmail
free.emailLoggingDesc: Log emails to Odoo with attachments
free.recordCreationDesc: Create leads, partners, tasks, and tickets
free.limitedRecordsDesc: View first 5 opportunities, tasks, and tickets
free.loadTimeDesc: See how fast the sidebar loaded

plus.advancedSearchDesc: Search across all opportunities, tasks, and tickets (up to 50 results)
plus.tabsDesc: Switch between contact and company information
plus.relatedContactsDesc: Find colleagues from the same company
plus.quoteLevelsDesc: Control how much conversation history to include when logging
plus.groupingDesc: Group opportunities, tasks, and tickets by stage or status
plus.emailParticipantsDesc: View Odoo contact information for all email participants
plus.recentActivitiesDesc: See recent messages from Odoo's conversations for each contact
plus.notificationsDesc: Identify and organize Odoo notification emails with module badges, mention detection, and quick-access filtering
plus.autologDesc: Link a Gmail thread to one or more Odoo records and have every new email logged automatically into their chatter (currently in private beta)
plus.multiCompanyDesc: Switch between the companies you have access to in Odoo, with cross-company contact lookup when an email matches a partner that lives in a different company

emailLogging.title: Email Logging
emailLogging.step1: Open the email you want to log
emailLogging.step2: View the contact in the sidebar
emailLogging.step3: Find the record you want to log to (opportunity, task, or ticket)
emailLogging.step4: Click the envelope icon next to the record
emailLogging.step5: The button changes to a green checkmark when logged
emailLogging.internalNoteDesc: Emails are logged as internal notes in Odoo's chatter. This means only internal users (employees) can see them. Portal users (customers) will NOT see logged emails. Notifications are only sent to internal followers of the record.
emailLogging.duplicateIntro: The extension has two layers of duplicate detection:
emailLogging.visualIndicatorDesc: Helps you identify at a glance which records already have this email
emailLogging.serverSideDesc: Odoo prevents duplicates even if the visual indicator has expired
emailLogging.importantDesc: Even without the visual indicator, you cannot accidentally create duplicates. Odoo's server validates every logging request and rejects duplicates.

autolog.title: Auto-logging
autolog.intro: Auto-logging links a Gmail thread to one or more Odoo records so that every new email in that thread is logged into the record's chatter automatically — no need to click the envelope icon for each reply.
autolog.betaDesc: Auto-logging is currently in private beta. It will be available to all PLUS subscribers soon. To request early access, contact support.
autolog.requirement1: PLUS plan (and beta access during private beta)
autolog.requirement2: Gmail API connection (granted from Settings > Auto-logging > Connect)
autolog.requirement3: Auto-logging toggle enabled in Settings > Auto-logging
autolog.activateTitle: How to Activate Auto-logging on a Thread
autolog.activateStep1: Open the email thread in Gmail and view the contact in the sidebar
autolog.activateStep2: Find the record you want to track (opportunity, task, ticket, or contact)
autolog.activateStep3: Click the sync icon next to the record. The icon turns green when active and a confirmation toast appears
autolog.activateStep4: From now on, every new email in this thread is logged automatically into the chatter of that record
autolog.forwardOnlyDesc: Auto-logging is purely forward-looking. Emails that already existed in the thread before activation are NOT logged retroactively. To log older emails, use the manual envelope button as usual.
autolog.whatGetsLoggedDesc: Each new email is added as an internal note in the record's chatter, with:
autolog.logged1: A Gmail-style header (From, To, Date, Subject)
autolog.logged2: The email body without quoted history
autolog.logged3: The original date of the email (not the time it was processed)
autolog.logged4: Linked partners — recipients are matched in Odoo by email and attached to the message
autolog.logged5: An "Auto-logged from Gmail" footer linking back to the extension
autolog.internalNoteDesc: Auto-logged emails are saved as internal notes (mt_note), not public messages. Only internal Odoo users can see them — portal users (customers) won't.
autolog.multipleTitle: Linking a Thread to Several Records
autolog.multipleDesc: You can activate auto-logging for the same thread on multiple records (for example, an opportunity and a task related to the same conversation):
autolog.multiple2: 1 thread → several records: every new email is logged on each linked record sequentially.
autolog.multiple3: The same record cannot be linked twice from the same thread — duplicates are rejected.
autolog.fromSidebarDesc: When the open thread has any active mappings, an "Auto-logging (N)" section appears at the top of the sidebar:
autolog.fromSidebar1: Click the section to expand it and see every record linked to the current thread
autolog.fromSidebar2: Click on a record's name to open it in Odoo
autolog.fromSidebar3: Click the ✕ button to deactivate auto-logging for that specific record
autolog.fromSettingsDesc: Open Settings > Auto-logging to manage every active mapping:
autolog.fromSettings1: Enable or disable auto-logging globally with a single toggle
autolog.fromSettings2: Connect or reconnect the Gmail API
autolog.fromSettings3: See the list of active mappings, the count, and the time of the last successful check
autolog.fromSettings4: Deactivate all mappings at once with the "Deactivate all" button
autolog.limit1Desc: Only emails that arrive after activation are logged. Existing emails must be logged manually.
autolog.limit2Desc: A mapping with no new email activity for 90 days is removed automatically. Activate it again to resume tracking.
autolog.limit3Desc: If the linked record is deleted in Odoo, the mapping is removed automatically the next time the extension tries to log into it.
autolog.limit4Desc: If the Gmail API connection expires or is revoked, auto-logging stops for all threads until you reconnect from Settings > Auto-logging.

relatedContacts.title: Working with related contacts
relatedContacts.intro: The Related Contacts section in the sidebar lists colleagues from the same company as the email's sender. From there you can take quick actions on each contact without leaving Gmail.
relatedContacts.openInOdooDesc: Click anywhere on the contact card — the avatar, the name, or the job title — to open that contact's record in Odoo in a new tab.
relatedContacts.composeTitle: Send a new email to a contact
relatedContacts.composeDesc: Click the email icon next to a contact's address. If you don't have any draft open, Gmail opens a new compose window with that address pre-filled in the To field.
relatedContacts.addRecipientTitle: Add a contact to an email you're already writing
relatedContacts.addRecipientDesc: If a Gmail compose window is already open when you click the email icon, the contact is added as a recipient of that draft instead of opening a new one. The extension chooses where: if the To field is empty, the contact goes there; if it already has someone, the contact is added to CC and the compose minimises so you can keep clicking other contacts to build the recipient list.
relatedContacts.copyTitle: Copy a contact's email or phone
relatedContacts.copyDesc: Click the email or phone text — not the icon next to it — to copy it to your clipboard. A small toast confirms the copy.
relatedContacts.callDesc: Click the phone icon to start a call. The action is handed off to whichever app your system has registered for phone numbers — your mobile dialer, FaceTime, Skype, Microsoft Teams, etc. If no app is configured, the click does nothing.

recentActivities.title: Navigating recent activities
recentActivities.intro: The Recent Activities section shows the latest messages and conversations from the contact's record in Odoo, so you can see what has been said without leaving Gmail.
recentActivities.timestampTitle: See exactly when an activity happened
recentActivities.timestampDesc: Each entry shows a relative date like "5 days ago". Hover the date to see the exact day and time, formatted in your browser's locale.
recentActivities.jumpTitle: Jump to a specific message in Odoo
recentActivities.jumpDesc: Click any entry in Recent Activities to open the related Odoo record in a new tab. On Odoo 18 and later, the click goes one step further: Odoo scrolls to that exact message and highlights it for you. On Odoo 16 and 17 the record opens normally — the click still saves you the navigation, you just have to scroll to find the message yourself.

opportunities.title: Managing Opportunities
opportunities.leadRecords1: Opportunities linked to the contact (by partner_id)
opportunities.creatingLeadsIntro: Convert an email conversation into an Odoo opportunity with one click:
opportunities.step1: Open the email you want to convert into a lead
opportunities.step2: View the contact in the sidebar
opportunities.happens1: The lead is created with the email subject as name
opportunities.happens2: Email body content is added as description
opportunities.happens3: The contact from the sidebar is linked
opportunities.happens4: The lead opens in Odoo in a new tab for additional editing
opportunities.importantDesc: The email is NOT logged automatically. To associate the email with the new lead, return to Gmail and click the envelope icon next to the opportunity.

tasksTickets.title: Tasks & Tickets
tasksTickets.taskRecords1: Tasks linked to the contact (by partner_id)

notifications.title: Notification Organization
notifications.intro: Organize Odoo notification emails directly in your Gmail inbox. The extension identifies notification emails from Odoo and adds visual badges so you can instantly see which module they belong to and whether you've been mentioned.
notifications.prerequisiteTitle: Prerequisite: Enable Email Notifications in Odoo
notifications.prerequisiteDesc: For this feature to work, your Odoo user must be configured to receive notifications by email (not in the Odoo inbox):
notifications.prerequisiteStep1: In Odoo, go to your Profile (top-right menu > My Profile)
notifications.prerequisiteStep3: Select "Handle by Emails" (instead of "Handle in Odoo")
notifications.prerequisiteNote: This ensures Odoo sends notification emails to your Gmail, where the extension can identify and organize them.
notifications.setupStep1Title: Find your notification email in Odoo
notifications.setupStep1Desc: Check a recent Odoo notification email in Gmail and look at the sender address (e.g., notifications@yourcompany.com). Alternatively, ask your Odoo administrator for the outgoing notification email.
notifications.setupStep2Desc: In the Gmail sidebar, click the menu button (three vertical dots) and select Settings.
notifications.setupStep3Desc: Go to the Notifications tab and paste the email address. The extension will start identifying notification emails immediately.
notifications.setupNoteDesc: If you're not sure which email Odoo uses for notifications, check a recent notification email in Gmail and look at the sender address.
notifications.moduleBadgesDesc: Color-coded badges appear next to notification emails in your inbox, showing which Odoo module triggered the notification:
notifications.moduleBadge5: And more: Purchase, Mailing, Timesheet, etc.
notifications.mentionBadgesDesc: When someone mentions you in an Odoo record (using @ or by name), a special badge appears on the notification email:
notifications.mentionUnreadDesc: You have an unread mention — you haven't opened this email thread in Gmail since the mention
notifications.mentionReadDesc: You've already seen this mention — you opened the thread in Gmail after being mentioned
notifications.mentionHowItWorksDesc: Mention detection is based on the messages stored in the Odoo record, not the email itself. The extension checks the record's chatter for @mentions or references to your name. This means:
notifications.mentionHowItWorks1: If you recently switched from "Handle in Odoo" to "Handle by Emails", older threads may show a "Mentioned" badge for mentions that happened before you started receiving emails — even though the mention won't appear in any Gmail email.
notifications.mentionHowItWorks2: The read/unread status is determined by whether you've opened the email thread in Gmail after being mentioned. Once you open the thread, the badge turns gray.
notifications.shortcutDesc: An "Odoo Notifications" shortcut appears in Gmail's left sidebar, below your labels. Click it to instantly filter your inbox and show only Odoo notification emails.
notifications.shortcutNoteDesc: The shortcut only appears after you configure a notification email in Settings > Notifications.
notifications.customizationDesc: Each notification feature can be toggled independently in Settings > Notifications:
notifications.customToggle1: Module badges — show/hide the color-coded module badges
notifications.customToggle2: Mention badges — show/hide the mention detection badges
notifications.customToggle3: Sidebar shortcut — show/hide the Odoo Notifications link in Gmail's sidebar

multiCompany.title: Multi-Company Support
multiCompany.intro: If your Odoo user has access to several companies, the extension lets you choose which one is active. The active company decides which records the sidebar shows you and which company every new lead, task, ticket or contact is created in.
multiCompany.requirement2: An Odoo user with access to two or more companies (in Odoo: Settings > Users > Allowed Companies)
multiCompany.selectorDesc: When you have access to more than one company, a building icon appears in the sidebar header, next to the settings (⚙) button:
multiCompany.selector1: Hover the icon to see the active company name ("Operating in {company}")
multiCompany.selector2: Click the icon to open a dropdown listing every company you have access to
multiCompany.selector3: Pick a company with the radio buttons. The dropdown closes and the sidebar refreshes with data from the new company
multiCompany.selector4: If you have access to only one company, the icon is not shown
multiCompany.persistDesc: Your selection is saved per Odoo instance, so opening Gmail again resumes the company you were last working in. Logging out clears the saved selection.
multiCompany.affectsDesc: Once you pick a company, every operation in the sidebar is scoped to it:
multiCompany.affects1Desc: Opportunities, tasks, tickets and partners listed in the sidebar are filtered to those visible in the active company
multiCompany.affects2Desc: New leads, tasks, tickets and contacts created from the sidebar are assigned to the active company
multiCompany.affects3Desc: Emails processed by auto-logging are posted in the company that owns the linked record (see the Auto-logging note below)
multiCompany.crossLookupIntro: If you open an email from someone who isn't in the active company but exists somewhere else in your Odoo instance, the sidebar tells you and offers actions instead of leaving you with an empty state.
multiCompany.crossContactTitle: The contact exists in another company
multiCompany.crossContactDesc: The sidebar shows the contact found, the company it lives in, and two actions:
multiCompany.crossContactAction1Desc: Removes the contact's company restriction so users in every company you have access to can see it. Asks for confirmation before saving
multiCompany.crossContactAction2Desc: Creates a separate contact record in the active company, leaving the original untouched
multiCompany.crossParentTitle: Only the parent company exists in another company
multiCompany.crossParentDesc: The contact is missing, but a related company (matched by email domain) exists in another of your companies. Two actions:
multiCompany.crossParentAction1Desc: Makes the parent company visible to every company you have access to, then lets you create the contact under it
multiCompany.crossParentAction2Desc: Creates an independent company record in the active company and the contact under it
multiCompany.shareWarningDesc: Sharing a contact or company across all your companies changes its visibility for every user with access to those companies. The extension asks for confirmation before saving.
multiCompany.autologDesc: When you activate auto-logging on a record, the extension remembers the company that record belongs to. New emails on that thread are always logged in the right company, even if you've switched the active company in the sidebar afterwards.
multiCompany.freePlanDesc: If you have access to several companies on the FREE plan, the extension uses your default company in Odoo for every operation. The selector icon does not appear, and cross-company lookups are not offered. Upgrading to PLUS unlocks both.
multiCompany.limit1Label: Not synced with the Odoo browser switcher
multiCompany.limit1Desc: The extension uses your API key, while the Odoo company switcher uses cookies. The two are independent — switching companies in your Odoo tab does not change the active company in Gmail and vice versa.
multiCompany.limit2Desc: If your administrator changes your default company in Odoo, the extension keeps using the previously cached value until you log out and log back in.
multiCompany.limit3Desc: The selector is single-select: the sidebar shows records from one company at a time. To see records from another company, switch to it.
multiCompany.limit4Label: Access error after creating a record
multiCompany.limit4Desc: When you create an opportunity, task, ticket or contact from the sidebar (or click "View in Odoo" on one), the extension opens the record in a new browser tab. Odoo loads it under whichever company is currently active in your Odoo browser session (the cookie session — see above). If that company differs from the one where the record was created, Odoo may show an access error. On the FREE plan, the extension always creates records in your default Odoo company — to see them, switch your Odoo browser tab to that same default. On the PLUS plan, keep the same company selected in both the sidebar selector and your Odoo browser tab. Odoo 18 and later automatically switch your active company when you open a record that belongs to another of your allowed companies, so this issue is mostly visible on Odoo 17 and earlier.

troubleshooting.title: Troubleshooting
troubleshooting.checkEnabledDesc: Go to chrome://extensions/ and ensure toggle is ON
troubleshooting.emailMismatchDesc: Update the email in Odoo or create a new contact
troubleshooting.authStep1: Verify your API key is correct in Odoo → Preferences → Account Security
troubleshooting.authStep2: Regenerate the API key if needed
troubleshooting.authStep3: Check that your Odoo URL is correct (including HTTPS)
troubleshooting.selfHostedIntro: When connecting to a self-hosted Odoo instance (custom URL), Chrome requires additional permissions. If the permission popup doesn't appear:
troubleshooting.permissionPopupDesc: Custom Odoo URLs (not *.odoo.com) require an additional Chrome permission to communicate with your server
troubleshooting.extensionConflictDesc: Ad blockers, privacy extensions, or popup blockers can prevent Chrome's permission dialog from appearing
troubleshooting.temporaryDisableDesc: Temporarily disable other extensions (especially ad blockers) and try connecting again. Re-enable them after granting permission
troubleshooting.selfHostedNoteDesc: This permission is only required once per Odoo URL. After granting it, the extension will work normally with your self-hosted instance.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: Q: What Odoo versions are supported?
faq.a1: Odoo 14.0 and higher are supported. Compatible with on-premise installations (Community/Enterprise), Odoo.sh, and Odoo Online (Custom plan only, as Standard and Free plans don't have external API access).
faq.q2: Q: Does it work with other browsers?
faq.a2: Currently, the extension only supports Google Chrome. Support for other browsers may be added in the future.
faq.a3: Yes. The extension only accesses emails you actively view and communicates securely with your Odoo instance via HTTPS. Your API key is stored in Chrome's secure storage.
faq.q4: Q: What's the difference between FREE and PLUS?
faq.a4: FREE includes basic features (contact viewing, email logging, limited records). PLUS adds advanced search, financial insights, related contacts, notification email organization, and customization options.
faq.q5: Q: Can I cancel my PLUS subscription anytime?
faq.a5: Yes, you can cancel anytime without penalty. Your subscription will remain active until the end of your billing period.
faq.q6: Q: Does the extension read all my emails?
faq.a6: No, the extension only accesses emails you actively view. It does not scan your entire mailbox or read emails in the background.
faq.q7: Q: Is my API key stored securely?
faq.a7: Yes, your API key is stored in Chrome's secure storage (chrome.storage.sync), which Chrome encrypts using your OS user account credentials.

help.title: Getting Help
help.feedbackDesc: Help us improve! Click the feedback button in the settings menu (⋮) to share your suggestions anonymously via a Google Form.
help.contactDesc: If you have questions or issues not covered in this guide:
help.includeVersion: Include your Odoo version and extension version
help.includeScreenshots: Describe the issue with screenshots if possible

subscription.title: Subscription Management
subscription.subscribeStep1: Click "Upgrade to PLUS" from the sidebar menu (three dots in the header)
subscription.subscribeStep2: Complete the checkout process at Polar
subscription.subscribeStep3: Receive your license key via email
subscription.activatingIntro: Enter your license key in one of these ways:
subscription.activateOption1: A modal will appear right after checkout - enter your license there
subscription.activateOption2: Go to Settings > Account > Activate License
subscription.activateOption3: Click "Upgrade to PLUS" again and select "I already have a license"
subscription.licenseInfoDesc: Your license works on up to 3 instances (different computers or browsers). It must be used with the same Gmail account used for purchase.
subscription.freeTrialDesc: PLUS includes a 14-day free trial. You won't be charged until the trial period ends. Cancel anytime before to avoid charges.
subscription.regionalPricingDesc: We offer fair pricing. Prices are automatically adjusted at checkout through a discount.
subscription.cancelIntro: You can cancel your PLUS subscription at any time:
subscription.cancelStep1: Go to the Polar customer portal (link in your purchase email)
subscription.cancelStep2: Log in with the email used for your subscription
subscription.cancelStep3: Click "Cancel Subscription" in your subscription details
subscription.cancelNote: Your PLUS features will remain active until the end of your current billing period.

hero.title: Catch Odoo–Shopify order mismatches at a glance
hero.subtitle: Open any order and instantly see whether Odoo and Shopify agree — field by field, with a clear verdict and a discrepancy breakdown. Read-only, no setup.
hero.badge: Zero config — auto-detects your Odoo instance from your session. No API keys, no code.

instant.title: Instant order verdict
instant.description: Open an order in Odoo or Shopify and the panel appears with a global verdict — match or mismatch — before you finish reading the page.

records.title: Field-by-field breakdown
records.description: Totals, taxes, quantities, line items and addresses compared side by side, with every discrepancy called out and explained.

emailLogging.title: Verdict based on what's invoiced
emailLogging.description: Compares Shopify's current order against Odoo's net invoiced amount (invoices minus refunds) — not the frozen sale order — so partial invoices and refunds don't trigger false alarms.

autoLogging.title: Refunds & returns in context
autoLogging.description: Refunds, returns and order edits are shown as context, never counted as discrepancies. You see why the numbers differ, not just that they do.

multiCompany.title: Zero configuration
multiCompany.description: Auto-detects your Odoo instance and database from your active session. No instances to register, no API keys, no options page.

fast.title: Read-only & safe
fast.description: The extension never creates, edits or deletes anything in Odoo or Shopify. It only reads — safe to run on production from day one.

demos.title: See It In Action
demos.subtitle: Hover over each demo to see the extension in action

search.title: Instant verdict
search.description: The comparison panel appears automatically when you open an order, with a global match/mismatch verdict.

createContact.title: Discrepancy breakdown
createContact.description: Expand any order to see totals, taxes, quantities and line items compared field by field.

companyTabs.title: Invoiced vs current
companyTabs.description: See Shopify's current order next to Odoo's net invoiced amount, so partial invoices never look like errors.

recentActivities.title: Refund & return context
recentActivities.description: Refunds and returns are surfaced as context next to the line they affect — not as false discrepancies.

switchContacts.title: Cancelled order detection
switchContacts.description: Cancelled orders are detected and flagged with a clear banner instead of a confusing mismatch.

setup.title: Zero-config autodetection
setup.description: Connects using your active Odoo session — the instance and database are detected for you.

pricing.title: Simple pricing
pricing.subtitle: Start with a 14-day free trial. No credit card to begin.

free.name: Solo
free.feature2: Verdict based on net invoiced amount
free.feature3: Refunds, returns & edits shown in context

plus.name: Agency
plus.feature3: Seat management via the customer portal
plus.feature5: Verdict based on net invoiced amount
plus.feature6: Refunds, returns & edits shown in context

hero.title: Simple, Transparent Pricing
hero.subtitle: Start free, upgrade when you need powerful features. No credit card required.

free.name: Free
free.description: Perfect for individual users getting started

plus.name: PLUS
plus.description: For power users who need advanced search and features
plus.feature1: Real-time search across opportunities, tasks & tickets
plus.feature2: View up to 50 records per section (vs 5 in Free)
plus.feature11: Notification email organization with module badges
plus.feature12: Auto-log Gmail threads to Odoo records
plus.feature15: Customizable quote depth when logging emails

team.name: Team
team.description: For teams that need centralized management

comparison.title: Detailed Feature Comparison
comparison.teamNote: Team plan includes all PLUS features plus centralized billing and seat management.

cta.title: Ready to supercharge your Gmail-Odoo workflow?
cta.subtitle: Start with our Free plan and upgrade when you need advanced features.

overview.title: Overview
overview.intro: Odoo Integration for Gmail ("the Extension") is committed to protecting your privacy. This privacy policy explains how the Extension collects, uses, and protects your information.

infoCollect.title: Information We Collect
infoCollect.gmailDataDesc: The Extension accesses the following information from Gmail when you explicitly open an email:
infoCollect.senderRecipientDesc: Used to search for matching contacts in your Odoo instance
infoCollect.emailSubjectDesc: Used when you choose to create opportunities, tasks, or tickets in Odoo
infoCollect.emailDateDesc: Used for logging emails to Odoo records
infoCollect.odooDataDesc: The Extension retrieves the following information from your Odoo instance:
infoCollect.odooData1: Contact information (names, emails, phone numbers, companies)
infoCollect.autoLoggingDataDesc: Auto-logging is an opt-in feature. The Extension only accesses this data after you grant Gmail access in Settings > Auto-logging and explicitly link a thread to an Odoo record. Once a thread is linked, the Extension uses Google's Gmail API to:
infoCollect.autoLoggingData1: Periodically check for new messages on the threads you have linked (background polling while at least one mapping is active)
infoCollect.autoLoggingData2: Read the subject, sender, recipients, date and body of those new messages
infoCollect.autoLoggingData3: Post them as internal notes in the Odoo records you linked
infoCollect.autoLoggingDataNote: The Extension only requests the gmail.readonly OAuth scope. It cannot send, modify, archive or delete emails. Auto-logging stops when you disconnect Gmail in Settings, deactivate the mapping, or remove the Extension. Tokens can also be revoked from your Google Account permissions at any time.
infoCollect.configDataDesc: The Extension stores your Odoo instance configuration locally:
infoCollect.configData5: Gmail OAuth tokens (only when auto-logging is enabled) — required to access the Gmail API on your behalf; revocable from Settings > Auto-logging or your Google Account
infoCollect.configData6: Auto-logging mappings (thread-to-record links and IDs of messages already logged) — stored locally for the duration of each mapping
infoCollect.configData7: Active company selection and the list of companies you have access to (multi-company instances) — cached locally to remember which company you are operating in

howWeUse.title: How We Use Your Information
howWeUse.intro: The Extension uses your information solely for the following purposes:
howWeUse.displayContactDesc: Show relevant Odoo contact details in Gmail's sidebar
howWeUse.createRecordsDesc: When you click "Create" buttons, the email content is sent to your Odoo instance to create opportunities, tasks, or tickets
howWeUse.logEmailsDesc: When you click "Log Email" buttons, the email content is saved to your Odoo instance's chatter
howWeUse.searchFilterDesc: Search through opportunities, tasks, and tickets associated with contacts
howWeUse.cachingDesc: Temporarily cache Odoo API responses locally to improve performance
howWeUse.autoLogDesc: When you enable auto-logging on a record, the Extension queries your Gmail account in the background to detect new emails on the linked thread and posts them as internal notes in your Odoo instance

storage.title: Data Storage and Security
storage.localOnlyDesc: All data is stored locally on your device using Chrome's storage APIs. No data is transmitted to any third-party servers except your own Odoo instance.
storage.transmissionDesc: The Extension communicates directly with YOUR Odoo instance using HTTPS. Your Odoo credentials and data are never sent to the extension developer or any third party.
storage.cacheDesc: Cached data is automatically cleared after 5 minutes or when you click the refresh button. You can also clear all cached data by removing the extension.

thirdParty.title: Third-Party Services
thirdParty.polarIntro: The Extension uses Polar (https://polar.sh/) for subscription management and payment processing. When you subscribe to the PLUS plan, Polar collects:
thirdParty.polar1: Your email address for account management and receipts
thirdParty.polar2: Payment information (processed securely by Stripe, not stored by the Extension)
thirdParty.importantDesc: Payment data is handled entirely by Polar and Stripe. The Extension never receives or stores your credit card information.
thirdParty.inboxsdkDesc: The Extension uses InboxSDK (https://www.inboxsdk.com/) to integrate with Gmail's interface. InboxSDK is a library that helps the Extension interact with Gmail's UI to display the sidebar and detect email contacts. InboxSDK does not collect, transmit, or store your email data.
thirdParty.posthogIntro: The Extension uses PostHog (https://posthog.com/) for anonymous usage analytics. PostHog helps us understand how the extension is used and identify issues. Data collected includes:
thirdParty.posthog1: Anonymous user ID (random UUID, not linked to your identity)
thirdParty.posthog3: Error reports for debugging (no personal data or email content)
thirdParty.posthog4: A boolean flag indicating whether your Odoo user has access to more than one company (used to know whether multi-company features are relevant). The list of companies and their names are NOT sent.
thirdParty.optOutDesc: You can disable analytics at any time in Settings > Account > Privacy.

dataSharing.title: Third-Party Data Sharing
dataSharing.noShare: We do NOT share your data with any third parties for advertising, analytics, or data brokering purposes.
dataSharing.yourOdooDesc: The Extension sends API requests to the Odoo instance URL you configure to retrieve and create records
dataSharing.polarDesc: Only for subscription management (email, subscription status, payment processing)
dataSharing.posthogDesc: Anonymous usage statistics (feature usage, errors). No PII or email/Odoo content. You can opt-out in Settings > Account > Privacy.
dataSharing.notSell: Sell your data to data brokers
dataSharing.notDeveloper: Send Gmail or Odoo data to the extension developer's servers

googleApi.title: Google API Services User Data Policy
googleApi.intro: Odoo Integration for Gmail's use and transfer of information received from Google APIs adheres to
googleApi.compliance1: The Extension only accesses Gmail data when (a) you explicitly open an email, or (b) you have explicitly enabled auto-logging and linked a thread to an Odoo record (a per-thread opt-in)
googleApi.compliance2: Email data is used exclusively for the disclosed purpose: integrating with your Odoo CRM
googleApi.compliance3: No Gmail data is used for serving advertisements
googleApi.compliance4: Gmail data is not transferred to third parties (except your own Odoo instance)
googleApi.compliance5: Gmail data is not used for determining creditworthiness or lending purposes
googleApi.compliance6: The Extension only requests the gmail.readonly OAuth scope. It cannot send, modify, archive or delete emails on your behalf

permissions.title: Permissions Explained
permissions.intro: The Extension requests the following Chrome permissions:
permissions.scripting: Required to inject the sidebar interface into Gmail
permissions.storage: Required to save your Odoo configuration and cache API responses locally
permissions.hostGmail: Required to access Gmail's interface and display the sidebar
permissions.hostOdoo: Required to communicate with Odoo Online cloud instances (e.g., yourcompany.odoo.com)
permissions.hostPolar: Required for subscription management and payment processing
permissions.hostParity: Required to offer Purchasing Power Parity (PPP) discounts based on the user's country
permissions.optionalIntro: The Extension requests optional permissions for custom Odoo instances:
permissions.optionalDesc: Used ONLY if you connect to a self-hosted Odoo instance on a custom domain (e.g., odoo.yourcompany.com or erp.example.com). This permission is only requested when you explicitly configure a custom Odoo URL.
permissions.optionalIdentity: Used ONLY when you opt into auto-logging. Lets the Extension complete the Google sign-in flow that grants Gmail API access (gmail.readonly scope). Requested at the moment you click "Connect" in Settings > Auto-logging.
permissions.optionalAlarms: Used ONLY when auto-logging is active. Lets the Extension wake up periodically in the background to check for new emails on the threads you have linked. Requested together with the identity permission.
permissions.noteDesc: The Extension only supports HTTPS connections for security. HTTP connections are not allowed.
permissions.whenRequestedIntro: Chrome will prompt you to grant optional permissions when:
permissions.whenRequested1: You configure a custom Odoo URL that is not an *.odoo.com domain
permissions.whenRequested2: You explicitly choose to connect to a self-hosted Odoo instance
permissions.canDenyDesc: if you only use Odoo Online (*.odoo.com). The Extension will work normally with Odoo Online without granting optional permissions.
permissions.whyIntro: Many organizations host Odoo on their own servers with custom domains (e.g., erp.acme.com, odoo.mycompany.net). To support these users, the Extension requests optional broad permissions. However:
permissions.why1: These permissions are ONLY used to communicate with YOUR configured Odoo instance
permissions.why2: They are NEVER used to access other websites
permissions.why3: You can revoke them at any time in Chrome settings
permissions.why4: The Extension will only request them if you enter a custom Odoo URL

rights.title: Your Data Rights
rights.intro: You have the following rights regarding your data:
rights.accessDesc: All your data is stored locally and in your Odoo instance, which you control
rights.deletionDesc: You can clear all Extension data by:
rights.deletion2: Removing the Extension from Chrome (clears all local data)
rights.portabilityDesc: Your data remains in your Odoo instance and is accessible via Odoo's export features
rights.controlDesc: You can revoke the Extension's access at any time by removing it

retention.title: Data Retention
retention.intro: The Extension retains data as follows:
retention.configDataDesc: Stored until you remove the Extension
retention.preferencesDesc: Stored until you remove the Extension
retention.uninstall: When you uninstall the Extension, all locally stored data is automatically removed by Chrome.

children.title: Children's Privacy
children.desc: The Extension is not intended for use by children under the age of 13. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children under 13.

changes.title: Changes to This Privacy Policy
changes.desc1: We may update this privacy policy from time to time. We will notify users of any material changes by updating the "Last updated" date at the top of this policy and, if required, through the Extension's update notifications.
changes.desc2: Continued use of the Extension after changes constitutes acceptance of the updated privacy policy.

contact.title: Contact Information
contact.desc: If you have questions or concerns about this privacy policy or the Extension's data practices, please contact:

legal.title: Legal Disclaimer
legal.desc1: Odoo Integration for Gmail is an independent extension and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by Odoo S.A. or Google LLC.
legal.desc2: Odoo is a trademark of Odoo S.A.
legal.desc3: Gmail and Chrome are trademarks of Google LLC.

header.title: Release History
header.subtitle: Track new features, improvements, and fixes in each version

v0402.bugFixes: Internal stability and error reporting improvements

v0400.notificationOrganizationDesc: A new set of tools to manage Odoo notifications in Gmail:
v0400.moduleBadges: Module badges (Task, Ticket, Opportunity...) appear on notification emails so you can identify them at a glance
v0400.mentionedBadge: "Mentioned" badge highlights emails where someone tagged you — red when unread, gray when read (requires Conversation View)
v0400.sidebarShortcut: Quick-access shortcut in the Gmail sidebar to filter all Odoo notifications
v0400.configurable: Fully configurable — enable or disable each notification feature independently in Settings
v0400.generalImprovements: General stability and user experience improvements
v0400.settingsScrollFix: Fixed settings dropdown not closing when scrolling the page

v0312.attachmentsDesc: Email attachments are now automatically uploaded when creating tasks, opportunities, and tickets

v0311.emailDateDesc: Original email date is now preserved when logging to Odoo chatter
v0311.contactDetectionDesc: Fixed contact detection after Gmail DOM changes (e.g., deleting a message from a conversation)

v0310.duplicateDetectionDesc: Smart duplicate detection when logging emails, showing who already logged it
v0310.emailLoggingDesc: Logged emails now appear correctly in Recent Activities
v0310.currencySymbolDesc: Fixed currency display when grouping opportunities by stage
v0310.odoo19ApiDesc: Fixed compatibility issues with Odoo 19.1's JSON API

v0300.globalSidebarDesc: Persistent sidebar icon that stays visible across Gmail, updating automatically when you switch emails
v0300.groupingDesc: Group opportunities, tasks, and tickets by project, stage, or team
v0300.emailParticipantsDesc: See and switch between all contacts in a conversation
v0300.recentActivitiesDesc: View the full interaction history with a contact from Odoo's conversations
v0300.uiRefreshDesc: Redesigned buttons and tooltips for a cleaner, more consistent interface

v0220.selfHostedDesc: Clearer guidance when additional permissions are needed for custom Odoo instances
v0220.securityDesc: Enhanced URL validation across the sidebar

v0211.attachmentsDesc: Fixed an issue where logging emails with attachments failed on Odoo 19+

v0210.odoo191Desc: Works seamlessly with the latest Odoo version
v0210.analyticsDesc: We now collect anonymous usage data to improve the extension. No personal information is collected. You can opt-out anytime in Settings.

v0200.freeTrialDesc: Try PLUS free for 14 days before being charged. Cancel anytime during the trial.
v0200.regionalPricingDesc: Automatic price adjustment based on your location through Parity Deals
v0200.newLanguagesDesc: Interface now available in Italian and Portuguese

v0120.versionDisplayDesc: See the extension version directly in the UI
v0120.localizedUserGuideDesc: User guide opens in the user language
v0120.feedbackButtonDesc: Easily send feedback from the settings menu to help us improve
v0120.toRecipientFixDesc: Fixed contact detection when sending emails to multiple recipients (TO + CC)

v0110.visualIndicatorDesc: See at a glance which emails have already been logged to a record
v0110.quoteLevelsDesc: Control how much conversation history to include when logging emails

v0100.multiLanguageDesc: Full interface translation (English, Spanish, French, German)
v0100.accessibilityDesc: WCAG AA compliant colors and keyboard navigation
v0100.rateLimitingDesc: Prevents HTTP 429 errors with request throttling
v0100.typescript: Architecture refactored to TypeScript with service layer

v0006.gmailFixDesc: Resolved "threadID element not found" error caused by Gmail UI changes

v0005.emailHeadersDesc: Email logs now display structured headers (from/to/date/subject) for better readability
v0005.duplicateDetectionDesc: Prevents accidentally logging the same email multiple times to a record
v0005.relatedContactsDesc: Now displays job titles and improved layout with icons for email and phone
v0005.conditionalTooltipsDesc: Tooltips only appear when text is truncated, reducing UI clutter
v0005.headerFixDesc: Settings and logout menu now always accessible during error states, preventing users from being stuck

v0004.odoo19Desc: Full compatibility with the latest Odoo version
v0004.searchBehaviorDesc: Clearing search now properly resets to initial view
v0004.setupInstructionsDesc: Enhanced guidance for API key configuration

v0003.minorFixes: Minor fixes and improvements to configuration setup

v0002.financialInsightsDesc: View financial metrics for your customers and suppliers directly in Gmail
v0002.visualEnhancementsDesc: Improved interface design and user experience

v0001.contactCompanyDesc: View Odoo contact and company details directly in Gmail
v0001.crmOpportunitiesDesc: View and create sales opportunities from your inbox
v0001.projectTasksDesc: View and create tasks associated with contacts
v0001.helpdeskTicketsDesc: View and create support tickets from emails
v0001.emailLoggingDesc: Save emails to Odoo records with one click, including attachments
v0001.advancedSearchDesc: Search across all your opportunities, tasks, and tickets
v0001.extendedViewsDesc: View up to 50 records per section (vs 5 in Free plan)
v0001.relatedContactsDesc: See other contacts from the same company

changelogPage.subscriptionRequiredDesc: Advanced features are available with a PLUS subscription. See pricing for details.

cta.title: Ready to try the latest version?
cta.subtitle: Get started with our Free plan or upgrade to PLUS for advanced features.

v0602.odoo17TasksDesc: Fixed the Tasks section failing to load on Odoo 17.
v0602.versionDisplayDesc: Closed tasks now stay hidden and contact fields load correctly, regardless of your plan or Odoo version.

v0600.cookieAuthDesc: Set up the extension using your active Odoo session — there's no need to generate and paste an API key anymore. An API key stays optional and is only required to enable background auto-logging.
v0600.apiKeyInSettingsDesc: You can now add or update the API key after setup, from the Connection group in Settings, to enable background auto-logging.
v0600.smartContactDesc: On threads with both a colleague and an external contact, the sidebar now picks the external one and shows who you're about to save in Odoo before you create the contact.
v0600.hideSectionsDesc: Company and Related contacts can now be turned off from the Personalization tab in Settings, just like the other sections.

v0521.odoo16FinancialDesc: Fixed an issue introduced in v0.5.2.0 that prevented the financial insights section from loading on Odoo 16 instances.

v0520.overdueBlockDesc: Financial Insights now highlights the overdue amount and invoice count for each contact. On Enterprise with Account Follow-up enabled, it also shows the next reminder date and follow-up level.
v0520.financialLabelsDesc: Renamed "Credit" to "Outstanding receivables" and "Debit" to "Outstanding payables" to match standard accounting terminology.
v0520.multiTenantSetupDesc: Configuration now works on multi-tenant Odoo hosts by propagating the database identifier in the authentication request.

v0510.quickContactActionsDesc: Copy email or phone numbers, or compose a new email, with one click from any contact in Related Contacts.
v0510.activityTimestampsDesc: Hover over a date in Recent Activities to see the exact time, and click to open the related record. On Odoo 18 and later, the click jumps straight to that specific message inside the record.
v0510.clearerSetupErrorsDesc: When the Odoo URL is invalid or sits behind a proxy that returns an HTML error page, the sidebar now shows an actionable hint instead of the raw HTML response.

v0502.financialOdoo16: Fixed an error that prevented the Financial section from loading customer data on Odoo 16.

v0501.dedupCrossFlow: More reliable duplicate detection between manual log and auto-log

v0500.autoLoggingDesc: Stop logging every reply by hand:
v0500.autoLoggingThread: Link a thread once and the next messages get logged automatically to your lead, task, ticket, or contact
v0500.autoLoggingMultiple: One email can leave a trace on several records at once (a lead AND a ticket, for example)
v0500.autoLoggingDistinguish: See at a glance which emails you logged yourself and which came in automatically
v0500.autoLoggingVisibility: Know at any time how many threads are being tracked
v0500.autoLoggingHealth: If something goes wrong, you'll see it in Settings before it becomes a problem
v0500.multiCompanyDesc: Work across all your Odoo companies without leaving Gmail:
v0500.multiCompanySwitch: Switch between your Odoo companies right from the sidebar header
v0500.multiCompanyCorrect: Create leads, tasks, and tickets without picking the wrong company — they always go to the active one
v0500.multiCompanyDuplicates: Avoid duplicate contacts: if a customer already lives in another of your companies, the extension warns you before creating a new one
v0500.multiCompanyOptions: Decide what to do when a customer's company is in another Odoo: share, replicate it here, or create a standalone contact

v0409.errorDetailsDesc: Error messages now include a 'Show details' option that reveals the technical server response, making it easier to report issues without opening browser developer tools.

v0408.contactNameFix: Fixed 'Unexpected error' when saving contacts from emails with a blank sender name

v0406.financialFieldsFix: Fixed Company section not loading for users without accounting permissions

v0405.errorClassification: Fixed error messages not showing the correct cause (e.g. invalid API key or connection timeout)

v0403.setupGuide: Added a help link in the setup form with a detailed guide for creating API keys across all Odoo versions (14-19)

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.subtitle: Set up in under a minute — no code, no API key, no configuration required

step1.title: Install
step1.description: Add the extension from the Chrome Web Store with one click

step2.title: Open an order
step2.description: Open any order in Odoo or Shopify. You're already signed in, so there's nothing to configure.

step3.title: Read the verdict
step3.description: The panel appears with a match/mismatch verdict and a field-by-field breakdown

howItWorks.sessionNote: Uses your existing Odoo session to detect the instance automatically. If it ever expires, the extension just asks you to sign back into Odoo — nothing else to manage.

socialProof.headline: Built for teams running Odoo and Shopify together through a connector

testimonials.title: What users are saying
testimonials.subtitle: Real feedback from teams using Odoo Integration for Gmail every day.

perryCaplan.quote: Solves so much in our email-heavy workflow.
perryCaplan.author: Perry Caplan

footer.title: Odoo–Shopify Order Check
footer.notAffiliated: Not affiliated with Odoo S.A. or Shopify Inc.
footer.odooTrademark: Odoo is a trademark of Odoo S.A.; Shopify is a trademark of Shopify Inc.
